category_title: Adolescent student with blindness and selective mutism who uses voice-output devices for communication
minimal_context: Sally and her instructor are sitting at a table during one-on-one instruction to use a second voice-output device.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Sally, I have a second voice-output device for you today. It is the big button communicator.

STUDENT: (Remains silent, pushes the device away from her and refuses to use it.)

TUTOR: Let's try this again. I am putting the device back on the table in front of you.

STUDENT: (Sits silently and continues to refuse to use the device.)

TUTOR: Please use the buttons to communicate a choice.

STUDENT: (Places her arm across all four buttons, rather than use the device appropriately to communicate a choice.)

options.A: Provide written feedback using a 3-point Likert-type scale ranging from 1 (needs improvement) to 3 (great job) on the student's daily progress report.
options.B: Conclude the lesson without pushing the student further, assuming that she is pretty much done with the communication activity for the session.
options.C: Place the device on the table, tell the student there is something interesting to show her, and guide her to explore using hand-under-hand assistance.
options.D: Remind the student of due dates and stress the need to complete the communication tasks in order to pass the subject for the term.
